I have not locked the content into a single track, so I’m open to shaping it as career advice, educational upskilling, or even broader personal development—whichever approach you feel will generate the strongest, most practical outcomes for this audience.

• A concise framework that maps out the topics and flow of the guidance (think modules, milestones, or sessions).

full_description:
# Logo & Brand Identity Design for SaaS Platform (NexBrix)

## Project Overview

NexBrix is a modern SaaS platform that helps businesses manage inventory, purchasing, suppliers, deliveries, workforce management, attendance, rostering, timesheets, reporting, and day-to-day operations from a single platform.

Our initial customers are cafés, restaurants, hospitality businesses, and multi-location operators, with future expansion into retail, warehousing, and service businesses.

We are looking for a branding designer to create a professional logo and visual identity that feels:

* Premium
* Sophisticated
* Modern
* Trustworthy
* Experienced
* Memorable

The brand should feel closer to Apple, Ramp, [login to view URL] and other modern software brands than traditional ERP or accounting software.

---

## Existing Direction

An initial concept has already been developed and will be shared with shortlisted candidates.

Current preferences:

* Wordmark-focused logo
* Clean and timeless typography
* Strong readability
* Elegant spacing and proportions
* Modern SaaS aesthetic
* Professional rather than playful

The final solution must work equally well on:

* Website
* Mobile app
* Social media
* Marketing materials
* Business documents

Brand name:

NexBrix

Preferred styling:

NexBrix

---

## What We Need

### 1. Logo Exploration

Provide at least 3 distinct logo concepts.

Examples may include:

* Pure wordmark
* Wordmark with subtle brand element
* Wordmark with supporting icon

We are open to creative direction.

---

### 2. Colour Exploration

We would like the designer to propose colour directions that align with the brand attributes above.

Please include multiple colour palette options and explain the thinking behind each direction.

---

### 3. Final Brand Assets

* Primary logo
* Secondary logo
* Monochrome versions
* Light and dark background versions
* App icon
* Favicon
* Social media icon

---

### 4. Mini Brand Guide

Include:

* Final colour palette
* Typography recommendations
* Logo clear-space rules
* Minimum sizing
* Usage examples

---

## Deliverables

Editable files:

* AI
* EPS
* PDF

Digital files:

* SVG
* PNG

---

## Revision Process

* Minimum 3 initial concepts
* Minimum 2 revision rounds
* Final source files transferred upon completion

---

## Timeline

Delivery required within 2-3 days.

---

## Proposal Requirements

Please include:

1. SaaS or technology branding examples.
2. Number of concepts included.
3. Number of revisions included.
4. Estimated delivery timeframe.
5. Confirmation that editable source files will be provided.

Shortlisted designers will receive the current concept and additional project information.

full_description:
Rive animator for a premium consumer wellness iOS app
I'm looking for a skilled Rive animator to create a set of around 22 interactive animations for a premium consumer wellness app for iOS. The brand is calm, elegant, and refined — warm tones, soft greens and cream — so the motion should feel "alive but quiet," never flashy or cartoonish.
The work falls into two groups:

UI & feedback micro-interactions — a score-ring reveal and gentle breathing, card and badge reveals, a loading shimmer, tab-bar selection, progress/confidence bars, an XP float, and similar polished interface motion.
A few signature hero pieces — a mascot character with multiple states, a growing plant that develops through stages, and a couple of other key animated elements.

Deliverables:

Clean, optimized .riv files (ideally under ~300 KB for vector animations), each with a well-structured state machine, inputs, and properties.
Source project files and clear integration notes/documentation for our iOS developers.
For the hero pieces, recreating the supplied reference images as clean vectors before animating — so you'd handle both the vector artwork and the animation.

What I'll provide: a detailed animation brief, reference images, and full specs for every animation (exact palette, motion behaviour, timing, and the feel I'm after), so you'll have clear direction from day one.
Requirements: strong proficiency in Rive (state machines, inputs, optimized exports), experience animating app/UI interfaces, an eye for premium and restrained motion, and clear communication. Everything is documented and ready to share, so a motivated animator can start quickly. Please include relevant Rive work from your portfolio.

full_description:
I want a fully-automated buying agent that can locate specific trading card products the moment they appear and complete checkout for me on specialized trading-card sites as well as other mainstream retailers I specify. The script should watch item pages or search results, detect restocks instantly, verify the price is within a threshold I set, and then execute the entire purchase flow—login, add to cart, shipping, and payment—without manual input.

Configurable fields (item URL or keyword, desired quantity, max price, billing/shipping profiles, scheduling) must sit in a simple JSON/YAML file or a small GUI so I can adjust targets quickly between drops. I need accurate status logging, basic error handling, and an optional Discord/webhook ping that confirms each successful order or flags a failure.

You’re free to pick the stack—Python with Selenium/Playwright or Node with Puppeteer are both fine so long as headless execution, proxy support, and captcha-solving hooks are possible. Clean, well-commented code and a short setup guide are the final deliverables; I should be able to run the bot on a Windows PC or Linux VPS with nothing more than the instructions you include.

full_description:
I have an existing e-commerce website hosted on Netlify. The website was created using Claude Design, and I need an experienced developer to integrate Stripe properly.
The website sells fitness clothing. The frontend is already built, but the payment and order system needs to be completed.
Scope of work
The developer must:
Integrate Stripe Checkout into the existing website
Connect the correct products, prices, sizes, colours and quantities to Stripe
Use Netlify Functions or another secure server-side solution
Make sure Stripe secret keys are never exposed in the frontend
Create working success and cancelled-payment pages
Set up Stripe webhooks to confirm successful payments
Create a reliable order flow that stores or sends all necessary order information
Make sure I receive the following information for every order:
Customer name
Email address
Phone number
Delivery address
Product purchased
Size
Colour
Quantity
Total amount paid
Stripe payment or order ID
Set up order confirmation emails for the customer
Set up order notification emails for the store owner
Test the full checkout process in Stripe test mode
Help switch the integration from test mode to live mode
Deploy the completed solution to Netlify
Make sure the integration works on both mobile and desktop
Shipping
The checkout should collect the customer’s delivery address. Please explain whether you can also integrate shipping prices based on country, order value or shipping method.
A future integration with PostNord or another shipping provider may also be required. Please state whether you have experience with this.
Important
I do not only need a Stripe payment button. I need a complete and reliable checkout and order-management flow, so I can clearly see what the customer ordered and where the order should be shipped.
The existing design and layout should not be changed unnecessarily.
Required experience
Stripe Checkout
Stripe webhooks
Netlify
Netlify Functions or serverless backend development
JavaScript or TypeScript
E-commerce checkout systems
Secure handling of API keys and environment variables
Transactional email systems such as Resend, SendGrid or similar
Deliverables
Fully working Stripe integration
Working checkout for all products and product variants
Secure webhook configuration
Customer order confirmation
Store owner order notification
Tested Stripe test-mode purchase
Live Stripe setup
Deployment to Netlify
Basic documentation explaining how the integration works
A short explanation of how I can update products and prices later
When applying, please answer:
Have you previously integrated Stripe with a Netlify-hosted website?
Can you show examples of similar work?
How will you handle Stripe webhooks and secure API keys?
How will order information be stored or delivered to me?
Can you set up confirmation emails for both the customer and store owner?
Is your price fixed, or can it increase during the project?
How many revisions and how much support after delivery are included?

full_description:
I want a self-contained Python script that runs on my Windows 10 PC (where Outlook 2010 is already installed and configured). When I launch the script it should:

1. Open Outlook, point straight to the Sent Items folder, grab the most recent message and place it in Forward mode. 
2. Strip “FW:” from the subject line. 
3. Remove the automatically generated header lines (“From:”, “Sent:”, “To:”, “Subject:”) that appear at the top of the body after a forward—everything else in the body stays exactly as is. 
4. Read Desktop\[login to view URL], take the first 250 email addresses, cut them from the file, and paste them into the BCC field of the draft. 
5. Send the email. 
6. Loop forever: once the message is sent, repeat the same cycle using the next 250 addresses that remain in [login to view URL] (the file will keep shrinking). If the file becomes empty the script can simply idle or close gracefully.

Key points 
• Must use standard Windows-friendly libraries (e.g. win32com / pywin32 for Outlook). 
• No manual interaction once the script starts—everything happens in code. 
• The original [login to view URL] file must be modified in place so that already-used addresses are removed. 
• Provide the .py file plus a brief README explaining any prerequisites and how to start the script. 

I will consider the job complete when I can run the script, watch Outlook send batches of 250 BCC recipients from my Sent Items thread, and see [login to view URL] steadily decrease until empty, with the subject and body edits applied exactly as specified.

I am looking for a genuine independent freelancer, not an agency or company, to help with SEO, local SEO, Google Business Profile, content marketing and social media management for two Australian businesses.

Please do not apply if you are an agency, digital marketing company, sales representative, account manager, or someone who will outsource the work to a team. I want to work directly with the actual person doing the work.

The two businesses are:

1. Intaglio 3D Crystals

An Australian online WooCommerce store selling personalised 2D/3D subsurface laser engraved crystal gifts, trophies, awards, keychains, memorial gifts, wedding gifts and corporate gifts.

Website: [login to view URL]

For this business, normal website SEO, WooCommerce SEO, product/category page optimisation, blog/content marketing and social media marketing are required.

2. Kumon Cranbourne / Kumon Education Business

This is a local Kumon education business physically operating from two locations.

Important: Kumon does not have our own fully controlled public website. We only have access to a Kumon portal/profile, which is not indexed like a normal website. Therefore, I do not want someone to sell me normal website SEO for Kumon unless they clearly understand this limitation.

For Kumon, the work is mainly:

Local SEO
Google Business Profile optimisation
Google Maps visibility
Local search visibility
Social media content
Parent-friendly education marketing
Review strategy
Local competitor research
Recommendations on how to use the non-indexed Kumon portal/profile for business benefit
Clear advice on what can and cannot be done without a normal public website

Kumon physically operates from two locations:

Location 1: Cranbourne, VIC
Location 2: Cranbourne North, VIC

The freelancer must understand how to handle a business with two physical locations and explain whether separate Google Business Profiles, landing pages, citations, social content or local SEO strategies are recommended.

Background

I have previously worked with a marketing provider and was not satisfied with vague reporting, unclear deliverables, lack of measurable outcomes, and unclear work completed.

This time I want a practical, hands-on freelancer who can show exactly what has been done each week/month.

I do not want generic reports, unclear SEO language, or promises without evidence.

No termination notice period.

No spammy backlinks, fake reviews, copied content, low-quality directory submissions or risky SEO methods.

All accounts, websites, social media pages, Google profiles, reports, designs, captions and content remain under my ownership.

WooCommerce SEO

full_description:
# Freelance Document Formatting & Editing Associate

## Job Overview

We are looking for detail-oriented freelancers to work on pre-formatted Word documents. Your role is simple: compare an original document with a formatted version, apply the required changes, and ensure the final document meets quality standards.

## Responsibilities

* Review the original document and the formatted document.
* Apply the specified changes and corrections accurately.
* Maintain the existing formatting and document structure.
* Perform basic proofreading and quality checks before submission.
* Make any additional corrections suggested by the Quality Control (QC) team and resubmit the document if required.
* Ensure all assigned documents are completed within the given timeline.

## Requirements

* Laptop or computer with Microsoft Word or equivalent software.
* Good attention to detail.
* Basic document editing and formatting skills.
* Ability to follow instructions accurately.
* Reliable internet connection for communication and file submission.

## Compensation

* ₹50 per document completed and approved.
* For freelancers completing more than 70 documents in a day, the rate increases to ₹100 per document for additional documents.
* Payment is based on successful completion and approval of the assigned work.

## Work Process

1. Receive the original document and the formatted document.
2. Review the required changes provided for that document.
3. Apply the changes to the formatted version.
4. Submit the completed document for QC review.
5. If any corrections are suggested, make the necessary changes and resubmit.

## Time Commitment

* Each document typically takes 10-15 minutes to complete.
* Flexible working hours.
* Work from anywhere.

This role is ideal for individuals with strong attention to detail who are looking for flexible freelance work.

full_description:
We’re looking for UGC creators, influencers, content creators, models, personal brands, and online professionals to test a new digital identity protection platform.

This is a simple beta testing project and should take around 5 minutes to complete.

You do not need to create a video, record a testimonial, or make polished content.

What is the platform?

- The platform is designed to help real people prove they are human online and protect their face, likeness, and personal brand from AI misuse, impersonation, and deepfakes.

- It allows creators to create a public verification profile showing that they are a real verified human, not an AI-generated account or fake profile.

- No government ID is required for this test.

Who this is for

This project is best suited to people who:

- Create content that includes their face
- Use platforms like TikTok, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, X, LinkedIn, OnlyFans, Fansly, Twitch, or similar
- Are building a personal brand
- Care about trust, authenticity, impersonation, AI misuse, or deepfakes
- Are comfortable completing a quick face-based liveness check

This is not suitable for faceless accounts.

What you’ll do

You will be asked to:

- Create a profile (1 minute)
- Complete a quick face-based human verification (1 minute)
- Receive your public verification link (30 seconds)
- Share the link on one of your social platforms or profile pages
- Provide short, honest feedback about your experience (questionnaire) 1 to 2 minutes

The feedback may be used to help improve the platform and support future product development, funding, or grant applications.

What you’ll get
Free lifetime early access to the platform
Early access to new features
The chance to help shape a real creator identity protection product
Potential future paid collaboration opportunities
Potential to earn referral commission as a partner

Important
This is an early-stage beta product, so it may not be fully polished yet.

We are looking for genuine creators, influencers, models, and personal brands who show their face online and understand why identity protection is becoming important in the AI era.

To apply, please include:
A link to one of your social profiles where your face is visible
The type of content you create
How often you post
Why a tool like this could be useful to you or your audience

If you’re a good fit, we’ll send you the platform link and short feedback questionnaire.

full_description:
My shoe label now needs a clean, instantly recognisable mark that will live on packaging, social media, and in-store displays. The aesthetic is firmly minimalist: flat lines, generous white space, and no decorative flourishes. I want to keep everything in a monochrome palette so the logo sits effortlessly on any background or material. 

Instead of a literal sneaker or boot, I picture an abstract shape—something geometric or fluid—that hints at motion and craft without spelling it out. The wordmark and icon should work together or apart, and both must stay sharp when scaled down for a heel tag or up for a billboard. 

Please design with vector tools such as Adobe Illustrator so the final artwork remains fully editable. 

Deliverables 
• Primary logo (icon + wordmark) in AI, EPS, PDF, SVG, PNG 
• Reversed and single-colour variants for dark and light backgrounds 
• Clear spacing and usage guidelines (short style sheet) 

I’ll give quick feedback on each draft and aim to wrap the project once the final files pass a simple print and digital test.

full_description:
I am developing a range of sous vide main-course recipes for hospital and home-care patients in India and I need a food-tech minded chef or nutritionist to help me finalise them.

Scope 
• All dishes must be cooked entirely (or at least finish-cooked) sous vide. 
• Cuisine style: fusion—Indian flavours or ingredients paired with complementary international techniques or seasonings. 
• Dietary targets: low-sodium and diabetic-friendly are non-negotiable. In addition, I want clearly labelled variants that hit different macronutrient goals: high-protein recovery meals, low-protein renal-safe versions, low-carb portions, and complex-carb-focused options for sustained energy. 
• Ingredients must be readily sourced in India and cost-effective for institutional kitchens.

Deliverables 
1. Ten complete recipes (PDF or DOCX) with: 
 – exact ingredient weights, sous vide temperature & time, and finishing instructions 
 – plating/portion notes for a 300 kcal and a 450 kcal serving 
 – full nutrition panel per serving and per 100 g (Calories, Protein, Carbs—simple vs complex, Fat, Sodium, Fibre, Net Carbs, GI estimate) 
2. A master ingredient matrix so hospitals can bulk-order efficiently 
3. Food safety/HACCP notes specific to sous vide holding and regeneration in Indian conditions

Acceptance Criteria 
• Every recipe passes <500 mg sodium per 300 kcal serving and maintains ≤12 g simple sugars. 
• Temperature and time tables follow USDA/WHO safety guidelines for the stated proteins. 
• Flavour profiles reflect at least one recognisable Indian spice or regional element while offering a global twist.

If you have prior experience designing clinical or diet-specific menus, especially with immersion circulators (Anova, Polyscience, etc.) and nutrition-analysis software such as Cronometer or NutriBase, your insight will be invaluable. I look forward to seeing how you can help make healthy, exciting sous vide meals accessible to patients across India.

My budget for the same is 1000 INR only

full_description:
**Title:**
Flutter / Go Developer Needed for Pre-Launch Marketplace App – Stripe Connect Experience Important

**Description:**
Hi,

We are looking for a reliable developer to help maintain, debug, and improve our pre-launch marketplace / live shopping app.

The project is already developed, but we need someone who can understand the existing codebase, fix bugs when necessary, improve current features, and support us as we prepare for launch.

Ideally, we would like to find someone who can become our main technical support person for the project and be available daily for communication, quick checks, improvements, and urgent debugging when needed.

**Current stack:**

* Flutter mobile app
* Go backend
* Supabase database
* Kotlin for the Android floating widget / overlay feature
* Client web checkout flow built with Go-rendered templates, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and [login to view URL]
* HTMX / [login to view URL] may also be used in some parts of the web checkout

**Important experience:**
We especially need someone who understands marketplace payment flows, ideally with Stripe Connect or similar systems.

Experience with the following is important:

* Stripe Connect
* Connected accounts
* KYC / onboarding flows
* Marketplace checkout logic
* Platform fees / commissions
* PaymentIntent flow
* Payment status handling
* Split payment logic or marketplace fund distribution

**What we need:**

* Maintain the existing project
* Fix bugs when they appear
* Improve existing features
* Help with Stripe Connect / marketplace payment logic
* Support the client checkout flow
* Review and understand the current backend and database logic
* Be available daily for communication and support when needed
* Communicate clearly and regularly
* Be comfortable working on an early-stage startup product before launch

**Budget:**
We are pre-launch and working with a small starting budget, roughly around $100–$150/month depending on workload and availability. We are looking for someone who can be involved on a regular basis to help maintain, improve, and support the project as we move toward launch. Budget can increase later if the collaboration works well and the project grows.

**Ideal profile:**

* Strong Flutter experience
* Experience with Go backend
* Comfortable with Supabase
* Experience with Stripe Connect or marketplace payments
* Basic Kotlin / Android native knowledge is a plus
* Comfortable with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and [login to view URL]
* Familiarity with HTMX / [login to view URL] is a plus
* Able to work independently
* Honest, responsive, and reliable
* Interested in joining an early-stage product before launch

Please apply only if you are comfortable with the starting budget, the early-stage nature of the project, and being available daily for communication or urgent support when needed.

When applying, please briefly tell us about your relevant experience, your availability, and whether you are comfortable with the starting budget.

Thank you.

full_description:
Earlier:
Dataverse only → Power BI semantic model → Paginated report
This worked because all required columns were coming from one supported model/source.
Now:
Dataverse + Azure Databricks columns → same Power BI semantic model → Paginated report
Paginated report is failing/blank because the semantic model now has a mixed source model, and the Databricks master columns may not be resolving properly through the semantic model when paginated report queries it.
Most likely causes:
The Databricks columns are not imported/refreshed properly in the semantic model
Power BI visual may show them due to local/desktop cache or DirectQuery behavior.
Paginated report reads the published semantic model, not your desktop file.
Relationship issue between Dataverse table and Databricks master table
If relationship is inactive, many-to-many, wrong key, or filter direction issue, paginated report DAX may return blank.
Paginated report query is still based on old Dataverse-only fields
After adding Databricks columns, the dataset query in Report Builder may need to be refreshed/rebuilt.
Composite model / DirectQuery issue
If Dataverse is Import and Databricks is DirectQuery, paginated report may behave differently while querying the semantic model.
Credentials/gateway issue for Databricks in Power BI Service
Dataset may publish, but service-side refresh/query may fail for Databricks credentials.

I want to launch a web-based marketplace that works much like Freelancer, but it will cater exclusively to two user groups: influencers and brands. The flow is straightforward—brands post campaigns or one-off collaborations, influencers browse and bid, and both parties communicate through a built-in messaging system until a deal is struck.

Core features I need developed:
• User profiles for both influencers and brands, each with tailored fields (audience size, niches, past collaborations for influencers; campaign details and budget ranges for brands). 
• A posting area where brands create projects and influencers can submit proposals or quotes. 
• A real-time messaging system tied to each project so negotiations stay organized.

The build is web-only for now, so please focus your technical approach on a responsive front end and a secure, scalable back end. 3. Basic admin dashboard or script so I can manage users and projects. 
4. Deployment instructions (and, if you can, a live staging URL).

If you can demonstrate previous marketplace or social platform experience, that will help me decide quickly.

full_description:
Our small LAN runs on Windows Server 2016 Standard with a handful of Windows 11 Pro workstations. A sudden power loss last weekend left two of those PCs throwing constant COM errors, and I am also seeing knock-on GPO and folder-redirection failures. The immediate pain point is the COM problem: custom line-of-business apps and standard Microsoft Office programs crash whenever the redirection faults surface.

I need someone to remote in, isolate the root causes, and return both machines to a clean, reliable state. That means:

• Diagnosing and repairing the COM subsystem issues that appeared after the outage 
• Verifying and correcting any GPO or folder-redirection misconfigurations tied to those errors 
• Scrubbing the event logs so only current, actionable events remain 
• Providing a short hand-off note that explains what you changed so we can keep things stable until our planned server refresh next year 

All work will be done during a mutually agreed maintenance window; I can supply VPN or direct-desktop access as required. If you are fluent with Windows Server 2016, Windows 11, Active Directory, and event-log forensics, this should be a concise engagement.

full_description:
Crypto Trading Infrastructure – Security Hardening & Stabilization Project
Overview

I am looking for an experienced backend/security engineer to audit, stabilize, and secure an existing multi-service crypto trading infrastructure running on Linux VPS servers.

The system is already operational and consists of:

multiple Python trading bots,
Flask/FastAPI-style dashboards,
Binance Spot + Convert integrations,
SQLite databases,
systemd-managed services,
multi-port architecture.

The project has evolved over time and now requires:

security hardening,
infrastructure cleanup,
architectural improvements,
and long-term stabilization.
Recent Incident

A critical security incident recently occurred:

an external internet scanner discovered an exposed HTTP API endpoint,
the endpoint allowed emergency-close actions without authentication,
this triggered forced sell operations on live positions.

The vulnerable chain was:
Public dashboard port → internal localhost API → Binance execution.

Immediate fixes were applied:

authentication added,
localhost restrictions added,
firewall hardening completed,
endpoint audit performed.

However, I now want:

an independent technical review,
architecture redesign recommendations,
and long-term stabilization/security improvements.
Current Environment
Infrastructure
Linux VPS servers (Hetzner)
Multiple public ports/services
Python-based services
systemd
SQLite
Binance APIs
Spot + Convert logic
REST APIs
Dashboard interfaces
Main Concerns
Security hardening
Reverse proxy architecture
Authentication centralization
Public port exposure
API protection
Session management
Reliability
Error handling
Logging
Service isolation
Deployment consistency
What I Need
Phase 1 – Audit
Review all exposed ports/services
Identify security risks
Verify authentication coverage
Review API architecture
Review firewall rules
Review localhost trust assumptions
Review logging and monitoring
Phase 2 – Hardening
Central reverse proxy (Nginx or equivalent)
HTTPS setup
Single authentication layer
Internal-only service ports
Improved session/auth architecture
IP restrictions and/or VPN access
Rate limiting
Secure admin routing
Phase 3 – Stability
Review service startup issues
Debug unstable bot instances
Improve deployment consistency
Reduce maintenance complexity
Improve recovery procedures
Important Notes
This is NOT a greenfield project.
Existing codebase already works partially and must be stabilized carefully.
Experience with crypto trading systems is strongly preferred.
Security awareness is critical.
I prefer structured, documented changes rather than rapid untracked modifications.
Required Skills
Python
Linux server administration
Nginx / reverse proxy
API security
Flask/FastAPI
systemd
Firewall/UFW
Binance API experience
Debugging distributed services
Infrastructure hardening
Communication Expectations

I value:

transparency,
documented changes,
clear communication,
and approval before major production modifications.

Please describe:

your relevant experience,
similar infrastructure/security work,
and how you would approach hardening and stabilizing this type of system.
